title: rosa canina f. marisensis
intro: Rosa canina f. marisensis, also known as the Dog Rose or Shetland Rose, is a flowering plant that is native to the Shetland Islands in Scotland and is a variant of the Rosa canina species. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high levels of vitamins C and E, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa canina f. marisensis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or by extracting the medicinal compounds using a solvent.
